title: Encapsulation, Properties, and Descriptors · Contract Workshop
summary: Protect borrower limits, item status, and identifier rules while keeping a small usable public API. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an invariant-protecting circulation model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use public APIs, leading-underscore conventions, and name mangling according to collaboration and subclass risks.
Use properties to preserve attribute-like access when validation, computation, compatibility, or read-only behavior is needed.
Keep validation and invariants consistent across constructor, property setter, methods, deserialization, and copying paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
